description: Fuel injector #3 failed open causing cylinder misfire
possible_causes: Injector solenoid failure; Contaminated fuel; Wiring harness damage; ECM driver fault
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check injector resistance; 2. Inspect fuel quality sample; 3. Verify ECM output signal; 4. Check wiring harness continuity

description: Fuel injector #5 intermittent delivery below threshold
possible_causes: Clogged injector nozzle; Low fuel pressure; Injector calibration drift
diagnostic_steps: 1. Run injector cut-out test; 2. Check fuel rail pressure; 3. Inspect injector nozzle; 4. Recalibrate injector trim

description: Turbocharger overboosting above 38 psi limit
possible_causes: Wastegate actuator stuck closed; Boost sensor fault; Turbo bearing wear
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check wastegate actuator movement; 2. Verify boost pressure sensor reading; 3. Inspect turbo shaft play; 4. Check exhaust backpressure

description: Turbocharger underboost detected during loaded operation
possible_causes: Exhaust leak pre-turbo; Wastegate stuck open; Air intake restriction; Turbo wheel damage
diagnostic_steps: 1. Inspect exhaust manifold gaskets; 2. Test wastegate actuator; 3. Check air filter restriction; 4. Inspect compressor wheel

description: Engine coolant temperature exceeding 210F threshold
possible_causes: Thermostat stuck closed; Radiator blockage; Water pump impeller wear; Low coolant level
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check coolant level; 2. Test thermostat operation; 3. Inspect radiator fins; 4. Check water pump flow rate; 5. Verify fan clutch operation

description: Coolant temperature sensor reading erratic causing derate
possible_causes: Sensor internal short; Corroded connector; ECM input circuit fault
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check sensor resistance vs temperature; 2. Inspect connector pins; 3. Verify ECM input voltage; 4. Swap with known good sensor

description: Engine oil pressure reading below 25 psi at operating temp
possible_causes: Oil level low; Oil pump wear; Pressure relief valve stuck; Worn main bearings
diagnostic_steps: 1. Verify oil level on dipstick; 2. Install mechanical gauge to cross-check; 3. Check oil pump relief valve; 4. Sample oil for metal content

description: Engine oil pressure critically low under load causing shutdown
possible_causes: Oil pump gear failure; Oil pickup tube obstruction; Crankshaft bearing failure
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check oil level immediately; 2. Install mechanical pressure gauge; 3. Drop oil pan and inspect pickup screen; 4. Check pump drive gear; 5. Analyze oil sample

description: DPF regeneration incomplete after three consecutive attempts
possible_causes: Excessive soot loading; DPF outlet temp sensor fault; Dosing valve malfunction; Frequent short duty cycles
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check DPF soot load percentage via ET; 2. Verify exhaust temp sensors; 3. Inspect diesel dosing valve; 4. Force manual regen and monitor

description: DOC inlet temperature not reaching regen threshold
possible_causes: DOC substrate deterioration; Inlet temp sensor drift; Fuel dosing insufficient; Exhaust leak before DOC
diagnostic_steps: 1. Verify DOC inlet/outlet temp readings; 2. Check fuel dosing injector; 3. Inspect DOC substrate visually; 4. Check for exhaust leaks

description: Crankcase pressure slightly above normal range
possible_causes: Worn piston rings; Clogged crankcase breather; Turbo seal leak
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check crankcase breather for blockage; 2. Perform blowby measurement; 3. Inspect turbo oil seals; 4. Run compression test

description: Crankcase ventilation system restricted causing elevated pressure
possible_causes: Breather element clogged; Breather valve stuck; Oil separator failure
diagnostic_steps: 1. Inspect breather element; 2. Check breather valve function; 3. Measure crankcase pressure; 4. Replace breather assembly

description: Main hydraulic pump output pressure 15% below specification
possible_causes: Pump swashplate wear; Control valve spool sticking; Pump compensator out of adjustment; Internal leakage
diagnostic_steps: 1. Perform pump flow test; 2. Check compensator setting; 3. Monitor pump case drain flow; 4. Check control valve spool

description: Main hydraulic pump catastrophic pressure loss
possible_causes: Pump shaft shear; Swashplate bearing failure; Drive coupling failure
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check pump drive coupling; 2. Measure pump case drain flow; 3. Inspect pump for metal debris; 4. Check hydraulic oil sample

description: Boom cylinder drifting down more than 50mm in 5 minutes
possible_causes: Cylinder piston seal worn; Holding valve leaking; Control valve spool leaking
diagnostic_steps: 1. Perform cylinder drift test with load; 2. Check holding valve; 3. Isolate cylinder from valve and retest; 4. Inspect piston seals

description: Minor stick cylinder rod seal weeping at full extension
possible_causes: Rod seal wear; Rod surface scratch; Cylinder tube scoring
diagnostic_steps: 1. Clean rod and inspect surface; 2. Check for rod scoring; 3. Monitor leak rate over shift; 4. Schedule seal replacement

description: Hydraulic main control valve response sluggish on boom circuit
possible_causes: Spool sticking from contamination; Solenoid weak; Pilot pressure low; Spring fatigue
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check pilot pressure at valve; 2. Test solenoid resistance; 3. Inspect spool for scoring; 4. Check hydraulic oil cleanliness

description: Pilot joystick control slightly inconsistent in fine mode
possible_causes: Pilot valve wear; Joystick linkage play; Pilot filter partially clogged
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check pilot pressure output range; 2. Inspect joystick linkage; 3. Replace pilot filter; 4. Recalibrate joystick

description: Hydraulic oil temperature exceeding 200F during sustained operation
possible_causes: Oil cooler fins blocked; Cooling fan malfunction; Oil viscosity incorrect; Internal bypass valve stuck
diagnostic_steps: 1. Clean oil cooler fins; 2. Verify cooling fan operation; 3. Check oil viscosity grade; 4. Test bypass valve; 5. Check ambient temp conditions

description: Hydraulic oil temperature critically high triggering auto-shutdown
possible_causes: Cooler failure; Fan drive failure; System relief valve cracking; Excessive internal leakage
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check cooler airflow immediately; 2. Verify fan drive operation; 3. Measure system relief valve setting; 4. Check pump case drain temps

description: System pressure spikes above relief valve setting intermittently
possible_causes: Relief valve contamination; Valve spring fatigue; Shock loading from operation
diagnostic_steps: 1. Test relief valve cracking pressure; 2. Inspect valve seat; 3. Check for operator-induced shock loads; 4. Replace relief valve cartridge

description: Hydraulic return filter bypass indicator showing near-limit
possible_causes: Filter element approaching capacity; Oil contamination elevated; Extended service interval
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check filter bypass indicator; 2. Sample hydraulic oil; 3. Replace filter element; 4. Note contaminant type if visible

description: Battery voltage dropping below 23V during cranking
possible_causes: Battery cell degradation; Loose battery cable; Excessive parasitic draw; Alternator undercharging
diagnostic_steps: 1. Load test each battery; 2. Check cable connections and torque; 3. Measure parasitic draw with machine off; 4. Test alternator output

description: Battery disconnect switch showing intermittent high resistance
possible_causes: Switch contact corrosion; Loose mounting; Internal spring fatigue
diagnostic_steps: 1. Clean switch contacts; 2. Measure contact resistance; 3. Check mounting bolts; 4. Cycle switch several times

description: Alternator output below 26V at 1500 RPM loaded
possible_causes: Voltage regulator fault; Worn brushes; Rotor winding fault; Belt slipping
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check belt tension and condition; 2. Measure output voltage at alternator; 3. Check regulator; 4. Inspect brushes; 5. Test rotor winding resistance

description: Alternator not charging causing system voltage collapse
possible_causes: Belt broken; Alternator bearing seized; Complete regulator failure; Wiring open circuit
diagnostic_steps: 1. Inspect drive belt immediately; 2. Check alternator for free rotation; 3. Test output at B+ terminal; 4. Check exciter circuit

description: ECM reporting intermittent communication fault on J1939 data link
possible_causes: ECM connector corrosion; CAN bus termination missing; Wiring chafe; ECM internal fault
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check ECM connector pins; 2. Verify CAN bus termination resistance; 3. Inspect J1939 wiring for chafing; 4. Monitor data link with Cat ET; 5. Try backup ECM if available

description: ECM primary processor fault causing engine derate to limp mode
possible_causes: ECM internal failure; Power supply voltage spike; Software corruption; Connector water intrusion
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check ECM power supply voltage; 2. Inspect connectors for moisture; 3. Attempt ECM flash reprogram; 4. Check all ECM ground circuits; 5. Replace ECM if reflash fails

description: Coolant level sensor reading intermittently low with full reservoir
possible_causes: Sensor probe fouled; Connector corroded; Coolant conductivity low
diagnostic_steps: 1. Inspect sensor probe for scale buildup; 2. Check connector pins; 3. Test coolant conductivity; 4. Clean or replace sensor

description: Hydraulic oil temp sensor offset reading 15F high vs actual
possible_causes: Sensor calibration drift; Connector resistance; Wiring splice resistance
diagnostic_steps: 1. Compare sensor reading to infrared thermometer; 2. Check connector pins; 3. Measure sensor resistance vs temp chart; 4. Replace sensor

description: Intermittent open circuit on engine harness causing random fault codes
possible_causes: Harness chafe at routing clamp; Connector back-out; Pin corrosion; Heat damage near exhaust
diagnostic_steps: 1. Perform wiggle test while monitoring Cat ET; 2. Inspect harness routing clamps; 3. Check connectors for backed-out pins; 4. Inspect harness near exhaust shields; 5. Repair or replace harness section

description: Cab dome light circuit intermittent due to connector vibration
possible_causes: Connector pin fatigue; Bulb socket corrosion; Ground circuit loose
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check bulb and socket; 2. Inspect connector at cab harness junction; 3. Verify ground circuit; 4. Repair connector

description: Starter motor engaging slowly with audible solenoid chatter
possible_causes: Starter solenoid contacts worn; Battery voltage low during crank; Starter ground cable resistance; Ring gear damage
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check battery voltage during cranking; 2. Test starter solenoid pull-in voltage; 3. Inspect starter ground path; 4. Check ring gear teeth

description: Starter relay clicking but not engaging starter motor
possible_causes: Relay contact wear; Control circuit voltage drop; Key switch fault
diagnostic_steps: 1. Bypass relay to test starter; 2. Measure relay coil voltage; 3. Check key switch circuit; 4. Replace relay

description: Forward clutch slipping under load in 2nd gear
possible_causes: Clutch disc wear; Low clutch pressure; Solenoid valve restriction; Clutch piston seal leak
diagnostic_steps: 1. Perform clutch pressure test; 2. Check transmission oil level; 3. Monitor clutch slip percentage in Cat ET; 4. Inspect solenoid valve; 5. Plan clutch pack inspection

description: Reverse clutch not engaging causing no reverse movement
possible_causes: Clutch piston seal failure; Solenoid stuck closed; Clutch plates welded; Low system pressure
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check transmission system pressure; 2. Test reverse solenoid activation; 3. Perform stall test; 4. Inspect clutch assembly

description: Torque converter stall speed 200 RPM above specification
possible_causes: Torque converter one-way clutch slipping; Stator damage; Fluid contamination
diagnostic_steps: 1. Perform stall speed test both directions; 2. Check transmission fluid condition; 3. Monitor converter outlet temp; 4. Compare to baseline stall speed

description: Torque converter lockup clutch engaging with slight shudder
possible_causes: Lockup clutch facing glazed; Solenoid modulation issue; Fluid degradation
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check transmission fluid quality; 2. Monitor lockup solenoid modulation via Cat ET; 3. Check converter outlet temperature; 4. Perform lockup apply test

description: Transmission shift solenoid A response time out of specification
possible_causes: Solenoid coil degradation; Valve spool contamination; Wiring resistance high
diagnostic_steps: 1. Test solenoid resistance; 2. Check solenoid connector; 3. Activate solenoid with Cat ET; 4. Inspect valve bore

description: Shift solenoid C showing marginal current draw during 3-4 upshift
possible_causes: Solenoid coil resistance drift; Connector pin corrosion; ECM driver marginal
diagnostic_steps: 1. Measure solenoid current with clamp meter; 2. Check connector condition; 3. Compare to adjacent solenoid readings; 4. Monitor shift quality

description: Transmission oil temperature approaching high limit during grade operation
possible_causes: Cooler restriction; Fan speed insufficient; Oil level low; Excessive clutch slip
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check transmission oil level; 2. Clean cooler fins; 3. Verify fan operation; 4. Monitor clutch slip rates; 5. Check cooler bypass valve

description: Transmission temperature reading erratic causing unexpected derates
possible_causes: Sensor failure; Connector water ingress; Wiring damage; ECM input fault
diagnostic_steps: 1. Compare sensor reading to infrared measurement; 2. Inspect connector for moisture; 3. Test sensor resistance curve; 4. Check wiring to ECM

description: Transmission system pressure 10% below specification
possible_causes: Pressure regulator wear; Oil pump wear; Internal leakage; Clogged suction screen
diagnostic_steps: 1. Perform system pressure test; 2. Check oil level and condition; 3. Inspect suction screen; 4. Test pressure regulator valve; 5. Check pump output

description: Transmission oil filter differential pressure approaching bypass
possible_causes: Filter element loading; Fluid contamination; Extended service interval
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check filter indicator; 2. Sample transmission oil; 3. Replace filter element; 4. Inspect old element for debris type

description: Intake air temperature sensor reading 10F offset from ambient
possible_causes: Sensor calibration drift; Heat soak from engine; Connector resistance
diagnostic_steps: 1. Compare to ambient thermometer; 2. Inspect sensor location for heat sources; 3. Check connector; 4. Replace sensor if offset confirmed

description: EGR valve position not matching commanded position
possible_causes: EGR valve carbon buildup; Actuator failure; Position sensor fault; Wiring issue
diagnostic_steps: 1. Command EGR open/close via Cat ET; 2. Inspect valve for carbon deposits; 3. Check actuator operation; 4. Verify position sensor signal; 5. Clean or replace valve

description: Swing motor case drain flow slightly above normal
possible_causes: Internal wear on motor gerotor; High hydraulic oil temperature; Contaminated oil
diagnostic_steps: 1. Measure case drain flow at operating temp; 2. Compare to service limit; 3. Check oil temp; 4. Sample hydraulic oil

description: Fuel level sensor reading erratically causing low fuel warning at half tank
possible_causes: Sensor float arm bent; Rheostat worn; Connector corrosion; Tank baffle interference
diagnostic_steps: 1. Visually check fuel level; 2. Monitor sensor output voltage sweep; 3. Inspect tank sender unit; 4. Check wiring and connector

description: Transfer case output bearing noise increasing under load
possible_causes: Bearing wear; Low lubricant level; Contaminated lubricant; Misalignment
diagnostic_steps: 1. Listen for bearing noise pattern; 2. Check transfer case fluid level; 3. Sample fluid for metal content; 4. Inspect for external leaks; 5. Schedule teardown if confirmed

description: Engine speed sensor signal showing occasional dropout at low RPM
possible_causes: Sensor air gap excessive; Tone wheel damage; Sensor cable chafe; ECM input marginal
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check sensor air gap to tone wheel; 2. Inspect tone wheel teeth; 3. Check sensor cable routing; 4. Monitor signal with oscilloscope
